signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Japan FinMin Katayama warns of decisive action on speculation; 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains USD/JPY bid at 159.81. Intervention risk on a break above 160.00; JPY COT short −65k. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 vs HV30 at 8.2% and ATM IV at 9.4%. 25d RR at −0.14; mild put skew despite bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal; CFTC LF delta turning positive would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: EUR leads G8 laggards (EUR −0.493% vs USD +0.505%, 7 pairs each; G8 spread: 0.998%). Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2% — 175bp carry advantage drives the divergence; EUR/USD at 1.1681.

signals.title: GBP/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: USD leads G8 (+0.505% avg, 7 pairs) while EUR lags (-0.493% avg, 7 pairs); G8 spread 0.998%. BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp; no carry advantage. Focus on USD strength vs EUR weakness.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 vs RBNZ hike 100% priced for 27 May. Short-squeeze risk elevated if RBNZ delivers hike; positioning stalling at extreme short levels. NZD/USD at 0.5847; 0.5900 is the next resistance on a potential unwind.
